question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `account.id=gte:0.0.1&account.id=lte:0.0.9999999` (a valid wide range) to pass the `primarySortField=true` check in the Bound constructor (since adjustedLower <= adjustedUpper), and then retrieve all NFT allowances across the entire account range, leaking all submitted allowance transactions in a single paginated scan?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `account.id=gte:0.0.1&account.id=lte:0.0.1` (equal bounds) to trigger the `hasEqualBounds()` path in `Bound.adjustLowerRange()`, which rewrites the range to a single EQ operator, and then use this to precisely target a specific spender account's submitted allowance transactions without the server treating it as a range query?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `account.id=gt:0.0.0` (lower bound just above zero) to cause `getAdjustedLowerRangeValue()` to return 1 (0+1), effectively starting the query from account 1 and scanning all submitted NFT allowance transactions from the beginning of the account namespace?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `account.id=lt:0.0.9999999` (upper bound only, no lower) to cause `getAdjustedLowerRangeValue()` to return 0 (lower is null), and `adjustUpperBound()` to return 9999998 (9999999-1), causing the repository to scan all accounts from 0 to 9999998 and return their submitted NFT allowance transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `account.id=lte:0.0.9999999` (upper bound only, no lower) to cause `adjustUpperBound()` to return 9999999 directly (LTE does not decrement), and `getAdjustedLowerRangeValue()` to return 0, causing the repository to scan all accounts from 0 to 9999999 and return all submitted NFT allowance transactions in that range?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `account.id=gte:0.0.1` without an upper bound to cause `adjustUpperBound()` to return `Long.MAX_VALUE` (upper is null), causing the repository to scan all accounts from 1 to Long.MAX_VALUE and return all submitted NFT allowance transactions, effectively dumping the entire allowance table?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `token.id=gte:0.0.1` without an upper bound and with a valid `account.id=gte:0.0.1` to cause the token Bound's `adjustUpperBound()` to return `Long.MAX_VALUE`, causing the repository to return all NFT allowances for all tokens from token 1 onward, leaking all submitted token allowance transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `limit=100&order=asc&account.id=gte:0.0.1` and then follow each `next` link in the response to enumerate all NFT allowance records in the system page by page, with each page containing 100 records, effectively performing an unbounded full-table scan of all submitted allowance transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `limit=100&order=desc&account.id=lte:0.0.9999999` and follow each `next` link to enumerate all NFT allowance records in reverse order, allowing the attacker to identify the most recently submitted allowance transactions and target high-value accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted `account.id` range that exactly matches the spender field of a known high-value account (e.g., a DEX contract), combined with `owner=true`, to retrieve all NFT allowances granted to that spender by any owner, exposing the full set of submitted allowance transactions that authorized that spender?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `owner=false` combined with `account.id=gte:0.0.1` to query all NFT allowances where the path account is the spender, and by iterating over all account IDs in the path, enumerate every account that has been granted NFT allowances via submitted transactions, building a complete map of the allowance graph?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a two-part `account.id` array (e.g., `account.id=gte:0.0.1&account.id=lte:0.0.100`) that passes the `@Size(max=2)` constraint, and observe that the Bound constructor processes both parameters to set lower and upper bounds, allowing the attacker to confirm that the maximum array size of 2 is the only constraint on the range width, enabling arbitrarily wide range queries over submitted allowance transactions?
